metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products in the treatment of lipid disorders

rules.rule_text: The primary goal of treating lipid disorders is to prevent cardiovascular morbidity and mortality. Until clinical trial data on morbidity/mortality are available, the SmPC must state that beneficial effects on mortality and morbidity have not been evaluated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Until clinical trial data are available, it should be specifically mentioned in the Summary of product characteristics (SmPC) that beneficial effects on mortality and morbidity have not been evaluated.
rules.exceptions: Not required for new HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors where class evidence exists; morbidity/mortality studies not foreseen for new statins

rules.rule_text: For medicinal products modifying lipid parameters other than LDL-C, demonstration of a positive clinical outcome in terms of morbidity and mortality is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: For medicinal products modifying lipid parameters other than LDL-C, demonstration of a positive clinical outcome in terms of morbidity and mortality is required.

rules.rule_text: A relative reduction in LDL-C level is acceptable as a primary efficacy endpoint in patients with primary hypercholesterolemia, provided claims are restricted to a lipid lowering effect.
rules.verbatim_quote: A relative reduction in LDL-C level is acceptable as a primary efficacy endpoint in patients with primary hypercholesterolemia, provided that claims in the label are restricted to a lipid lowering effect.
rules.exceptions: Claims beyond lipid lowering require outcome data

rules.rule_text: An isolated effect on TG or HDL-cholesterol is not expected to be the sole basis for demonstrating efficacy. It should be seen in conjunction with the effect on non-HDL cholesterol and underlying pharmacological mechanisms.
rules.verbatim_quote: In principle, an isolated effect on TG or HDL-cholesterol is not expected to be the sole basis for the demonstration of the efficacy of a new lipid-modifying agent, but should be seen in conjunction with the effect on non-HDL cholesterol and the underlying pharmacological mechanisms of actions

rules.rule_text: Imaging modalities (cIMT, IVUS) lack evidence to be considered surrogate markers for outcomes. They cannot be the sole basis for an indication in adults.
rules.verbatim_quote: However they lack the evidence base to suggest that small changes in these parameters influence outcome (that is, to be considered as surrogate markers). ... At the present time, in adults, it is difficult to envisage an indication solely based on use of these imaging markers
rules.exceptions: Can be used in developmental phase (Phase II/III) as supportive parameters

rules.rule_text: When imaging markers are included to support an indication, the company must demonstrate the link between the marker, clinical event, and the influence of the intervention on imaging measures in the chosen patient population.
rules.verbatim_quote: When such markers are included in the clinical trials or an attempt is made to base the indication on these parameters, the onus rests with the company to demonstrate the necessary link between the marker, clinical event and the influence of the therapeutic intervention on imaging measures in the chosen patient population.

rules.rule_text: Imaging data from two different vascular beds by two different techniques is considered more robust for estimating overall atherosclerotic burden. Regression of atherosclerotic burden is the preferred parameter of effect.
rules.verbatim_quote: data generated from two different vascular beds by two different techniques is considered more robust in estimating the overall atherosclerotic burden. Demonstration of regression of atherosclerotic burden is the preferred parameter of effect rather than lack of progression as the endpoint.

rules.rule_text: The preferred primary endpoint for CV morbidity/mortality trials is a composite of major cardiovascular events (CV or all-cause death, non-fatal MI, and non-fatal stroke) adjudicated by a blinded, independent committee.
rules.verbatim_quote: the preferred primary endpoint should be a composite of major cardiovascular events (CV or all-cause death, non-fatal myocardial infarction and non-fatal stroke) adjudicated by a blinded, independent committee.
rules.exceptions: If CV mortality chosen instead of all-cause mortality, effects on non-cardiovascular mortality must also be taken into account

rules.rule_text: Softer endpoints (TIA, silent MI, unstable angina, need for PCI) are not encouraged as they are less objectively defined and can complicate interpretation. If included, clinically relevant justifications must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: The inclusion of such softer endpoints, which are less objectively defined can complicate interpretation of the results, and is accordingly not encouraged. If included, clinically relevant justifications should be provided.

rules.rule_text: Lipid-altering effects must be documented as pre-/post-treatment change under standardized, fasting conditions following a dietary lead-in period.
rules.verbatim_quote: Lipid-altering effects of lipid-modifying agents should be documented as the pre-/post- treatment change in lipid levels. All measurements should be performed under standardised, fasting conditions following a dietary lead-in period with or without wash-out of appropriate duration
rules.exceptions: Wash-out depends on pharmacological action of administered standard therapy

rules.rule_text: In patients with primary hypercholesterolemia, reduction in LDL-C is the primary endpoint. Changes in TG and HDL-C should be studied as secondary parameters.
rules.verbatim_quote: In patients with primary hypercholesterolemia reduction in LDL-C is the primary endpoint to support the indication of hypercholesterolemia or mixed hyperlipidaemia. ... Changes in TG, and HDL-C should also be studied as secondary parameters

rules.rule_text: Non-HDL-C can serve as a valid secondary endpoint in certain conditions, such as hypertriglyceridemia with diabetes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Estimation of non-HDL-C can also serve as a valid secondary endpoint in certain conditions, e.g. hypertriglyceridaemia with diabetes.

rules.rule_text: In diabetic subjects, pre/post treatment change in glycaemic control must be documented as it may affect lipid levels.
rules.verbatim_quote: In diabetic subjects pre/post treatment change in glycaemic control should be documented, as this may affect lipid levels.

rules.rule_text: For cIMT measurements, images of right and left common carotid arteries (CCA), carotid bulb, and internal carotid arteries (ICA) must be obtained. The primary measurement should be change in mean maximum IMT across 12 pre-selected carotid arterial segments over 18-24 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: For cIMT, images of right as well as left common carotid arteries (CCA), carotid bulb and internal carotid arteries (ICA) need to be obtained. ... It is recommended that the change in mean maximum IMT be the primary measurement across 12 pre-selected carotid arterial segments over time (18 - 24 months; as a study of shorter duration will neither be conclusive nor helpful).
rules.exceptions: If fewer segments are chosen, adequate justification including consensus reports is required

rules.rule_text: For IVUS, a minimum of 20% luminal narrowing of the relevant coronary artery at baseline is required. Percent plaque volume (change from baseline) is the recommended primary measurement.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to demonstrate changes with IVUS using a pullback method, a minimum of 20% luminal narrowing of the relevant coronary artery at baseline is required. ... For IVUS, percent plaque volume (change from baseline) is recommended as the primary measurement.

rules.rule_text: Investigative staff must receive comprehensive training, be blinded to treatment and sequence, and image acquisition/analysis must be carried out by experienced technicians. Centralized laboratory measurement is recommended.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3 Assessment of vascular damage (target organ damage)
rules.verbatim_quote: it is important that the investigative staff receive comprehensive training and those reading the images are blinded to treatment and sequence. Image acquisition and analysis should be carried out by experienced technicians to a high, reliable quality. ... A centralised laboratory measurement is recommended

rules.rule_text: Both genders must be adequately represented in the study population. Subjects 75 years and older in pivotal trials must be sufficient to assess both efficacy and safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: Both genders should be adequately represented in the studied population. ... The number of subjects 75 years and older included in (pivotal) trials should be sufficient to assess both efficacy and safety in this group.
rules.exceptions: Children/adolescents below 18 addressed in separate paediatric addendum

rules.rule_text: Patients for clinical outcome evaluation should have a well-characterized risk level and be either homogeneous or stratified by risk. Patients with atherosclerosis and/or type 2 diabetes must be represented in adequate numbers.
rules.verbatim_quote: patients should be chosen with a well characterised risk level and either homogeneous or stratified based on risk level, thus permitting a straightforward extrapolation of the results. Patients with clinical and/or other manifestations of atherosclerosis and/or type 2 diabetes mellitus should be represented in adequate numbers

rules.rule_text: Familial hypercholesterolemia patients (heterozygous and homozygous) must be studied in separate clinical trials if such indication is claimed.
rules.verbatim_quote: When specifically claimed, patients with familial hypercholesterolemia (heterozygous and homozygous) should normally be studied in separate clinical trials, based on their cholesterol levels and clinical genetic characteristics.

rules.rule_text: A dietary lead-in period is obligatory before randomization in all studies. Dietary supplements must be recorded and remain unchanged throughout the trial.
rules.source_section: 6. Strategy and design of clinical trials
rules.verbatim_quote: In all studies a dietary lead-in period is obligatory before randomization. ... Dietary supplements should be recorded and remain unchanged throughout the trial duration.

rules.rule_text: Patients on existing lipid-modifying therapies must be withdrawn with an adequate wash-out period when monotherapy is studied.
rules.source_section: 6. Strategy and design of clinical trials
rules.verbatim_quote: For patients administered similar or other lipid-modifying therapies, these should be withdrawn at the start of this period when monotherapy is studied, requiring an adequate wash-out.
rules.exceptions: Not applicable when studying add-on combination therapy

rules.rule_text: Off-target effects (e.g., blood pressure, immunological reactions, complement activation) noted in early studies must receive specific attention during Phase 2 or 3.
rules.verbatim_quote: When off target effects are noted in early, they will need specific attention during subsequent phase 2 or 3 studies.

rules.rule_text: Special attention must be paid to PK interactions with concomitant medications commonly used in lipid disorder populations. Genetic polymorphism studies may be required in subpopulations.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention should be paid to pharmacokinetic interactions with concomitant medications used commonly in these populations. In certain cases specific studies in subpopulations may be required to evaluate variations due to genetic polymorphisms relating to both efficacy and safety.

rules.rule_text: Dose-response studies must be randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blinded, with at least 3 dosages. Duration may vary from 4 weeks to 3 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dose-response studies should be randomized, placebo-controlled and double-blinded and at least 3 dosages should be studied to establish the clinically useful dose-range as well as the optimal dose. ... Duration of these studies may vary from 4 weeks to 3 months.

rules.rule_text: Placebo-controlled monotherapy trials are no longer acceptable in large groups of patients and high-risk subjects given established statin efficacy and safety. Statin-intolerant patients should be studied separately.
rules.source_section: 6.3.2.1 Demonstration of lipid-modifying effects as monotherapy
rules.verbatim_quote: Given the efficacy and safety of particular drugs (mainly statins), placebo controlled trials investigating products for monotherapy are no longer acceptable in large groups of patients and high risk subjects. Patients who are considered intolerant to statins due to adverse events should be studied separately
rules.exceptions: Short-term 3-arm studies including placebo may be valuable in initial therapeutic studies

rules.rule_text: Statin intolerance must be documented as evidence of intolerance due to emerging AEs to 2 different statins administered in doses required to achieve the target LDL-C level.
rules.source_section: 6.3.2.1 Demonstration of lipid-modifying effects as monotherapy
rules.verbatim_quote: There is no consensus definition for statin intolerance, but there should be documented evidence of intolerance due to emerging AEs to 2 different statins (administered in doses required to achieve the target LDL-C level).

rules.rule_text: Pivotal study dose must be justified by dose-finding results. Duration must be at least 3 months (known mechanisms) and preferably up to 12 months (novel mechanisms).
rules.source_section: 6.3.2.1 Demonstration of lipid-modifying effects as monotherapy
rules.verbatim_quote: The dose schedule selected for pivotal studies on lipid altering effects must be justified on the basis of the dose finding studies in the target population. Duration will depend on their expected outcome but should last at least a minimum of 3 months (for known mechanisms of action) and preferably up to 12 months (for others)

rules.rule_text: Combination lipid-modifying agents must be specifically studied vs. placebo in patients with inadequate response to individual components. For LDL-C elevations, patients should be on maximum-tolerated statin dose before adding a second agent.
rules.source_section: 6.3.2.2 Demonstration of lipid modifying effects in combination
rules.verbatim_quote: Combination of lipid-modifying agents should be specifically studied in comparison to placebo in patients with inadequate response to any of the components of the combination separately. ... Specifically, in cases of LDL-C elevations, patients should be on a maximum-tolerated statin dose, before adding a second lipid-modifying agent.

rules.rule_text: Combination strategies are not expected to be licensed as first-line therapy on the basis of their effect on LDL-C and other lipid parameters alone, unless benefit in terms of morbidity and mortality is justified.
rules.source_section: 6.3.2.2 Demonstration of lipid modifying effects in combination
rules.verbatim_quote: In principle, combination strategies are not expected to be licensed as first line therapy on the basis of their effect on LDL-cholesterol and other lipid parameters, in particular TG and HDL-C alone, unless the applicant is able to justify the benefit of such strategy in terms of morbidity and mortality.
rules.exceptions: Acceptable if morbidity/mortality benefit is justified

rules.rule_text: Clinical outcome benefit claims must be supported by long-term, controlled, parallel, double-blind studies. Non-inferiority approach requires establishing assay sensitivity.
rules.source_section: 6.3.2.3 Demonstration of benefits in clinical outcome
rules.verbatim_quote: Any claims of a beneficial effect on the clinical outcome, in particular cardiovascular outcome, should be supported by long-term, controlled, parallel and double-blind clinical studies. Either superiority or a non-inferiority approach can be adopted. When using the non-inferiority approach, establishing assay sensitivity is of paramount importance.
rules.exceptions: Placebo-controlled superiority study acceptable if no established therapy for specific target population

rules.rule_text: All adverse events must be fully documented with separate analysis of adverse drug events/reactions, dropouts, deaths, and laboratory results. An overall safety plan must be designed prospectively by Phase II.
rules.verbatim_quote: All adverse events occurring during the course of clinical trials should be fully documented with separate analysis of adverse drug events/reactions, dropouts, deaths while on therapy and clinical laboratory results. In the clinical studies, an overall plan for the detection and evaluation of potential adverse events ... should be prospectively designed early during the clinical development, optimally by the time of phase II studies.

rules.rule_text: Liver function tests must be routinely measured. Information on patients with different degrees of liver impairment (Child-Pugh Classification) must be included in the regulatory submission.
rules.verbatim_quote: Liver function tests should be routinely measured and analysed in line with accepted guidelines. Information on patients with different degrees of liver impairment (Child-Pugh Classification) should be included in the regulatory submission dossier.

rules.rule_text: Muscle symptoms must be actively sought and CK levels monitored regularly as part of safety evaluation. Myopathy/muscle toxicity must be defined using standard MedDRA query (SMQs). Post-marketing surveillance and risk management plans must be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that muscle symptoms should be actively sought in the development programme/clinical trials and CK levels be monitored as part of safety evaluation regularly. ... Myopathy/muscle toxicity should be defined using standard MedDRA query (SMQs) throughout the clinical development programme.

rules.rule_text: The MAA must adequately characterize the cardiovascular safety profile, especially for products with new mechanisms of action or drug classes where CV safety is not established.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that the drug development programme, containing all relevant clinical and non-clinical data, adequately characterizes the cardiovascular risk profile enabling an evaluation of the cardiovascular safety in the marketing authorisation application (MAA). This refers in particular to products with a new mechanism of action or products belonging to a drug class for which the cardiovascular safety profile is not yet established or questioned

rules.rule_text: Subjects above 65 and 75 years must be adequately represented in studies. The number of older subjects in pivotal trials must be sufficient to assess both efficacy and safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: Subjects above 65 and 75 years should be adequately represented in the studies, taking appropriate care of issues relating to therapeutic choices, fitness to participate in the trial and overall general health. ... The number of older included in (pivotal) trials should be sufficient to assess both efficacy and safety in this group.
